evidences.title: There is a new most retweeted tweet of all time and it's depressingly ...
evidences.content: It was posted on 5 January by Yusaku Maezawa, the founder of the Japanese online clothing retailer Zozotown, and it promises that he’ll personally pay one million yen ($9,200) to 100 people who follow and retweet him before 7 January. It’s currently on 3.8m retweets, which is a lot isn’t it. That’s it. Nothing special. Nothing clever. Just a ‘retweet and win some cash’ tweet, like a crap Facebook ‘like and share comp’ that you have virtually no chance of winning. Get exclusive shortlists, celebrity interviews and the best deals on the products you care about, straight to your inbox. [...] Thus, in an example of the all-too-rare things that are good about the internet, everyone on Twitter got behind him, helping him towards his ludicrous target of 18 million retweets which he did not reach but, with the publicity generated by his record-breaking 3.55m endorsements, he achieved his dream anyway. Of course, because nothing is ever truly pure and good, he let us all down a year on from getting his freebies by only eating a paltry 400 nuggets from his unlimited supply - a truly derisory effort that he should be ashamed of. [...] I mean, of course, why wouldn’t you enter it. Why would...

evidences.title: Billionaire Yusaku Maezawa responsible for the most retweeted tweet ever
evidences.content: Japanese billionaire Yusaku Maezawa. For the honor, the founder of Japan's second-largest online shopping site Zozo Inc. will be paying out a total of 100 million yen ($1.19 million) to 100 lucky people who shared the post. Seeking to be one of the randomly chosen winners, Maezawa's audience retweeted his post a record 3.8 million times, and gave it more than 900,000 "likes." The previous record holder for retweets was a college student in the U.S. whose goal was to get a year's free supply of chicken nuggets from the Wendy's fast-food franchise, according to Statista. That post had 3.58 million retweets and 990,000 likes. Ellen DeGeneres's 2014 Oscars selfie with Hollywood stars including Meryl Streep, Julia Roberts, Brad Pitt and Angelina Jolie was second with 3.3 million retweets. [...] Maezawa, who was relatively unknown outside Japan until recently, has sought to raise his global profile to make Zozo a leading global clothing brand within a decade. In September, he was introduced as the first passenger on a flight to the moon scheduled for 2023, on a rocket built by Musk's SpaceX. In October, Maezawa tweeted that he purchased a 1717 Stradivarius violin. Maezawa made a splas...

evidences.content: We got this ð pic.twitter.com/VkMPwJo9GI — World Record Egg (@egg\_rt\_record) January 14, 2019 The post said: ‘Let’s set a world record together and get the most retweeted tweet on Twitter. ‘Beating the current world record held by Yusaku Maezawa @yousuck2020 (5.3 million!) We got this.’ The Instagram egg managed to get over 40 million likes on the picture sharing app in around 10 days. METRO GRAB INSTAGRAM Egg beats Kylie Jenner as most liked picture on Instagram Jenner’s photo of her newborn daughter Stormi held the previous record with 18 million likes. The retweet record is currently held by Japanese billionaire Yusaku Maexawa, who racked up almost 5.3 million retweets on January 5. [...] # Copycat egg picture tries to steal Twitter record Martine Berg Olsen A copycat account is trying to make another picture of an egg the most retweeted tweet ever. The mystery page appeared after an almost identical image managed to break Kylie Jenner’s world record title for the most liked photo on Instagram this week. The ‘World Record Egg’, which tweets under the handle Egg\_rt\_record, claims no affiliation with its Instagram lookalike and is trying to beat the current Twitter world...

evidences.title: How to find the most liked tweets on Twitter - Tweet Binder
evidences.content: ### Barack Obama | 3.8 million likes As mentioned above, Elon Musk’s Coca-Cola tweet is the second most liked Twitter post. But that aside, the next on this list is a tweet by former US President Barack Obama. With 3.8 million likes, this poignant message from Obama rings true for people globally. ### Greta Thunberg | 3.7 million likes Activist Greta Thunberg’s scathing reply to Andrew Tate’s provocation on Twitter is next with 3.7 million likes. This post quickly went viral, thanks to the well-aimed humor and sarcasm. ### Joe Biden | 3.7 million likes US President Joe Biden’s tweet on his inaugural day as the President of the United States of America comes in next with 3.7 million likes. The positive tweet from President Biden saw a lot of interaction and engagement on Twitter. [...] Try to analyse his account yourself. It is free of charge. ## The most liked tweets of all time Many people obsess over how to find most liked tweet by user on Twitter. Tweets about big celebrities or those made by famous public figures are usually the most liked ones. Here is a list of the 5 top liked tweets of all time. evidences.title: What the biggest ever tweets say about Twitter on its 15th birthday
evidences.content: Monica Stephens is an assistant professor in geography at the University of Buffalo and analyses social media to understand trends in society, including politics. “What I think is evident from those lists is the changing demographics and geography of Twitter over the last 15 years,” she told The Independent. “The platform is substantially more global and has more users now than ever before, which is why the most liked tweets are all within the last four years and the majority from the last year. “It’s really nice that the most-liked tweets are very human.” The top retweeted posts of all time are topped by a pair from 2019 by Japanese billionaire Yusaku Maezawa, who promised to share $925,000 between 100 randomly selected people who shared it. [...] “Kobe was a legend on the court and just getting started in what would have been just as meaningful a second act,” he tweeted. “To lose Gianna is even more heartbreaking to us as parents. Michelle and I send love and prayers to Vanessa and the entire Bryant family on an unthinkable day.” Actor Andy Milionakis secured the fifth most-liked tweet ever when he posted about the SpaceX demo flight launch, with two NASA astronauts onboard. T...

evidences.title: Twitter/X Timeline System Design: Real-Time Feed, ...
evidences.content: Sitemap Open in app Sign in Sign in # Twitter/X Timeline System Design: Real-Time Feed, Reposts, and Ranking at Scale Rabin Pantha Rabin Pantha 5 min readJan 18, 2026 Twitter/X is the definition of a “live” product. When something breaks, trends spike instantly. A single tweet from a celebrity can trigger millions of reads, thousands of replies, and a wave of reposts within seconds. That “real-time feel” is not accidental — it’s the result of a timeline system designed for freshness, scale, and reliability. In this post, we’ll design a Twitter/X-like Home Timeline using a structured approach: Clarify requirements Define APIs Sketch high-level architecture Discuss storage design Explain core components and flows Add high availability and finalize [...] ## Non-Functional Requirements These define production-grade behavior: Low latency - Fast first paint for timeline: serve first page quickly Real-time updates - New tweets should be visible quickly (seconds for most users) High availability - Timeline must keep working during spikes and partial failures Scalability (read-heavy) - Timeline loads dominate traffic - Celebrity accounts create extreme fanout skew Consistency expectation...

evidences.content: Stacey McLachlan Stacey McLachlan is an award-winning writer and editor from Vancouver with more than a decade of experience working for print and digital publications. She is editor-at-large for Western Living and Vancouver Magazine, author of the National Magazine Award-nominated 'City Informer' column, and a regular contributor to Dwell. Her previous work covers a wide range of topics, from SEO-focused thought-leadership to profiles of mushroom foragers, but her specialties include design, people, social media strategy, and humor.
